title: Dry Peruvian Chicken Skewers
description: A peppery and snappy post-workout Peruvian dish featuring orange blossom water, this recipe is perfect for those looking for an anti-inflammatory friendly meal option.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, orange blossom water, garlic powder, black pepper, turmeric, ginger, and salt.
Add the chicken pieces to the bowl and toss to coat evenly.
Thread the chicken pieces onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Place the skewers on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Dehydrate the chicken in the oven for 3 hours, or until dry and slightly crispy.
Remove the skewers from the oven and let cool completely.
Serve the dry Peruvian chicken skewers chilled or at room temperature.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
